mission.title: the-update-framework-tuf-: Should we accept metadata that includes unrecognised fields?
mission.description: Should we accept metadata that includes unrecognised fields?. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check python-tuf installation
steps.description:
Verify your python-tuf installation:
```bash
pip show tuf
python3 -c "import tuf; print(tuf.__version__)"
```
Install if needed: `pip install tuf`. TUF (The Update Framework) is a Python library for secure software updates, not a Kubernetes workload.

steps.title: Review the issue context
steps.description:
**Description of issue or feature request**:

The current reference implementation will ignore unrecognised fields in a metadata file when loading it. Furthermore, some TAPs (i.e.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Should we accept metadata that includes unrecognised fields?
steps.description:
Please fill in the fields below to submit a pull request.  The more information
that is provided, the better.
Even though, this ADR documents something already implied in the TUF
spec in [document formats](https://theupdateframework.github.io/specification/latest/#document-formats)
it seems better to document this decision clearly so that it could be
referenced and give an explanation why someone can load a metadata file
with additional unrecognized fields.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description:
Test that the fix is working as expected:
```bash
python3 -c "import tuf; print(tuf.__version__)"
pip show tuf
```
Confirm the feature described in "Should we accept metadata that includes unrecognised fields?" is functioning correctly.
